skill.name: aeon
skill.description: Use Aeon for time series machine learning with scikit-learn compatible Python APIs. It guides classification, regression, clustering, forecasting, anomaly detection, segmentation, similarity search, feature extraction, distance metrics, datasets, and benchmarking for temporal data.
skill.summary: Guide time series machine learning workflows with the Aeon Python toolkit.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found a documentation-only skill with Markdown examples for the Aeon Python toolkit. The command execution, dynamic import, weak cryptography, hardcoded URL, system reconnaissance, environment access, and combined critical heuristic findings are false positives or benign examples. No prompt injection, credential access, hidden network exfiltration, or executable skill code was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer treated Markdown inline code and fenced Python examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These are documentation examples, not executable skill code, and no user-controlled shell invocation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown code blocks or inline API names. The repository contains documentation files, and no executable wrapper invokes these snippets automatically.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Python Imports Flagged as Dynamic Imports
low_findings.description: Static script findings point to ordinary Python import statements inside documentation code blocks. They describe Aeon modules and do not perform JavaScript-style dynamic imports or runtime code loading by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence shows static import statements in fenced examples. There is no loader code, eval path, or instruction to import untrusted modules.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Time Series Terms Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography alerts were caused by time series and machine learning terminology, such as descriptions of Aeon methods and distance metrics. No cryptographic implementation or weak hash use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines describe the skill and Aeon task categories. A targeted search found only one benign hashing term for similarity search, not password storage or cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Links Flagged as Network Risk
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL findings are public documentation and project links for Aeon. They are not network calls, do not transmit local data, and do not reference suspicious collection endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs point to official Aeon documentation, GitHub, examples, and API reference pages. No fetch, webhook, credential, or upload behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Benign Example: Dataset Download and GPU Environment Settings
low_findings.description: Some documentation examples can download public benchmark datasets or set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES if a user runs them. This is a user-visible operational effect, not hidden skill behavior or credential access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples are explicit and limited to public datasets or local GPU selection. The risk is low because execution requires the user to choose to run the example.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Critical Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The critical heuristic combined documentation snippets, public URLs, and code examples into a code execution plus network plus credential access pattern. Review found no credential access, no exfiltration destination, and no executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only network evidence is documentation links and dataset loader examples, and the only environment evidence selects a GPU. No secret reads or outbound data transfer were found.

content.user_title: Apply Aeon to Time Series Machine Learning
content.value_statement: Standard machine learning tools often miss temporal structure in sequential data. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use Aeon for specialized time series modeling workflows.
actual_capabilities: Select Aeon algorithms for classification, regression, clustering, forecasting, anomaly detection, and segmentation.
Guide data formatting for Aeon collection inputs with samples, channels, and timepoints.
Recommend feature extraction methods such as ROCKET, Catch22, and preprocessing pipelines.
Explain temporal distance metrics including DTW, LCSS, ERP, and Euclidean variants.
Use Aeon dataset and benchmarking references for reproducible model comparisons.
Help compare speed, interpretability, accuracy, and resource tradeoffs across Aeon methods.
limitations: The skill provides guidance and examples; it does not include executable automation files.
Users must install Aeon and required optional dependencies in their own Python environment.
Large deep learning models may need significant data, GPU resources, and tuning time.
Some dataset loader examples may download public benchmark data when run by the user.

use_cases.title: Classify sensor sequences
use_cases.description: Choose Aeon classifiers, prepare collection-shaped inputs, and compare fast baselines against higher accuracy models.

use_cases.title: Study temporal patterns
use_cases.description: Find anomalies, motifs, change points, and similarity relationships in physiological, industrial, or experimental signals.

use_cases.title: Build repeatable pipelines
use_cases.description: Combine Aeon transformers, scikit-learn estimators, validation splits, and benchmarks for production-oriented time series workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Starter Model
prompt_templates.prompt: I have labeled time series data. Help me choose an Aeon classifier, explain the input shape, and suggest a simple evaluation plan.

prompt_templates.title: Detect Anomalies
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Aeon references to design an anomaly detection workflow for one long time series. Include window size guidance and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Forecasting Methods
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare Aeon forecasting options for seasonal and nonseasonal data. Recommend baselines, metrics, and when to use neural forecasters.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Benchmark Study
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan an Aeon benchmark for several time series classifiers. Include dataset loading, resampling, metrics, statistical tests, and result interpretation.

output_examples.input: I need to classify vibration signals from machines.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend fast convolution-based baselines first, then distance-based or deep learning methods if accuracy or pattern complexity requires them.

output_examples.input: My series has rare spikes and possible regime changes.
output_examples.output: The skill would separate point anomaly detection from segmentation, suggest suitable Aeon methods, and propose visual checks before trusting alerts.

output_examples.input: I want a fair comparison across several datasets.
output_examples.output: The skill would outline dataset loaders, stratified resampling, standard metrics, and post-hoc tests for comparing model performance.

best_practices: Normalize or impute time series before modeling when the selected algorithm is scale sensitive.
Start with fast baselines before moving to ensembles or deep learning models.
Use validation splits, benchmark datasets, and appropriate temporal metrics for model selection.
anti_patterns: Do not use deep learning models first when the dataset is small or poorly labeled.
Do not ignore Aeon input shape requirements for samples, channels, and timepoints.
Do not compare algorithms without consistent splits, metrics, and preprocessing.

faq.question: What is Aeon used for?
faq.answer: Aeon is used for time series classification, regression, clustering, forecasting, anomaly detection, segmentation, similarity search, and feature extraction.

faq.question: Does this skill run Aeon automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples that users can apply in their own Python environment.

faq.question: Which data format does Aeon expect?
faq.answer: Many collection tasks use arrays shaped as samples, channels, and timepoints.

faq.question: Can it help with forecasting?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes baseline, statistical, regression-based, and neural forecasting options from Aeon.

faq.question: Does it support anomaly detection?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers point, subsequence, collection, and streaming anomaly detection approaches.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed files are documentation, and the static security findings were not confirmed as malicious behavior.
